readme:
### What it does

Apple Developer Docs MCP gives your AI agent comprehensive access to the Apple Developer Documentation ecosystem. 18 tools cover API search, framework browsing, WWDC session discovery, sample code retrieval, platform compatibility checks, and cross-reference resolution.

Your agent can find the right SwiftUI modifier, check UIKit API availability across platforms, browse WWDC sessions by topic and year, retrieve working sample projects, and discover related APIs — all without leaving the conversation.

### Tool coverage

| Domain | Tools | What you can do |
|--------|-------|-----------------|
| Documentation | 4 | Search docs, get page content, list technologies, search framework symbols |
| WWDC | 7 | Browse topics, list videos/years, get video detail, find related sessions, get code examples, search content |
| API Analysis | 4 | Platform compatibility, related APIs, similar APIs, batch reference resolution |
| Discovery | 3 | Documentation updates, technology overviews, sample code browsing |

### Source coverage

- **Apple Developer Documentation** — all frameworks across iOS, macOS, watchOS, tvOS, visionOS
- **WWDC Sessions** — videos from 2012 onwards with topic categorisation
- **Sample Code** — complete Apple sample projects with framework filtering
- **Technology Overviews** — getting-started guides, architectural overviews, best practices

### No auth required

All queries hit public Apple Developer APIs — no API keys, developer account, or tokens needed. Install and use immediately.

### Use cases

- **API lookup during development** — find the right API, check its signature, see platform availability
- **WWDC research** — discover sessions on a topic, find related talks, extract code examples
- **Migration planning** — check deprecations, find modern replacements, compare platform support
- **Learning new frameworks** — browse technology overviews, sample projects, and guided tutorials

setup.blurb: Apple Developer Documentation, WWDC sessions, API reference, and sample code. No credentials needed.
setup.help_text: No configuration required. All queries use public Apple Developer APIs. Install and use immediately.

scenarios.label: Research a SwiftUI API for implementation
scenarios.body: Search for the SwiftUI API, get its full documentation, check platform compatibility across iOS/macOS/visionOS, then find sample code demonstrating its usage.

scenarios.label: WWDC session deep-dive
scenarios.body: Browse WWDC topics, find sessions on a framework, get code examples from the session, then discover related sessions to build a learning path.

scenarios.label: Cross-platform development reference
scenarios.body: Look up Apple framework APIs for the iOS/macOS side, then search NixOS options and packages for the server-side infrastructure supporting the app.

certification.security_notes: Read-only. No credentials. All queries to public Apple Developer APIs (developer.apple.com). No write operations. No local filesystem access.

notes: Community listing for kimsungwhee/apple-docs-mcp (1225 stars). Read-only Apple Developer documentation reference. 18 tools covering docs, WWDC, sample code, and API analysis. Zero auth. First plugin to implement documentation-v1 contract with full conformance.
